task_completion.description: Format the title to be bold and minimum 14pt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Soil Survey and Management Assessment' at the top of the document is bolded and visibly larger than the body text.

task_completion.description: Include a Table of Contents at the beginning
task_completion.visual_evidence: A generated Table of Contents block is visible at the beginning of the document, listing the sections and page numbers.

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 1 styles to main sections
task_completion.visual_evidence: Main sections like 'Executive Summary', 'Site Description and Land Use History', and 'Management Recommendations' are formatted with a distinct Heading 1 style (larger, bold, or specific font).
Subsections like 'Ap Horizon (0-28 cm)' and 'Macronutrient and pH Analysis' are formatted with a distinct Heading 2 style, visually subordinate to Heading 1.

task_completion.description: Format body text to be justified and minimum 11pt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Paragraphs (e.g., 'The subject parcel is located...') have straight, even edges on both the left and right margins, indicating justified alignment.

task_completion.description: Convert tabular data into actual tables
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data for soil horizon properties, chemical analysis, and management recommendations are formatted inside actual word processor tables with visible gridlines or cell structures, rather than plain text separated by spaces or tabs.
The Page Style/Page Setup dialog is shown adjusting margins, or the top/side rulers in the word processor visibly reflect modified margin boundaries.
The save icon is clicked, a save dialog is completed, or the document title bar shows no 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used a word processor GUI (like LibreOffice Writer) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Writer application open with the document, rather than terminal commands injecting XML or using pandoc.

integrity.description: The tables are genuine word processor table objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Table tools/menus are visible when interacting with the data, or the tables have distinct cell borders, proving they are not just text aligned with the spacebar or tab key.

integrity.description: The original text and data were preserved during formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Keywords like 'Argiudoll', 'Clarion', and 'silty clay loam' are still visible in the document; the agent did not replace the text with a generic template.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Document path: /home/ga/Documents/soil_survey_report.odt. Expected Title: 'Soil Survey and Management Assessment'. Expected H1 sections: 'Executive Summary', 'Site Description and Land Use History', 'Soil Profile Descriptions', 'Chemical and Physical Analysis Results', 'Soil Classification and Taxonomy', 'Management Recommendations', 'Appendix: Methods and Standards'. Expected H2 subsections: 'Ap Horizon (0-28 cm)', 'Bt1 Horizon (28-56 cm)', 'Bt2 Horizon (56-91 cm)', 'Macronutrient and pH Analysis', 'Micronutrient and Physical Properties'. Body text samples to check for justified alignment: 'The subject parcel is located in Story County', 'Soil cores were extracted using a hydraulic probe', 'Management practices should focus on building'. Keywords that must remain in the text: Argiudoll, Munsell, CEC, Olsen, silty clay loam, organic matter, base saturation, Clarion.
